Currently, the only 2026 nominee on the platform is the Norwegian beauty-horror film “The Ugly Stepsister,” nominated for Best Makeup and Hairstyling. (A very entertaining take on an old fairytale that’s worth a watch if you can stomach tapeworms, IMO.)

Instant cinema classics like “Sinners” (which leads with a record-breaking 16 nominations) and “One Battle After Another” are streaming on HBO Max, for example.

Curious about what to watch in addition to the Oscars on Hulu? Check out what’s hitting the platform this month in our Hulu March 2026 roundup.

Oscars on Hulu FAQ

Can I watch the 2026 Oscars live on Hulu?

Yes, all Hulu subscribers can stream the 98th Academy Awards live as they air on ABC. You no longer have to wait until the next day to watch the ceremony on demand.

What time do the Oscars start on Hulu?

The live broadcast begins at 7:00 p.m. ET on Sunday, March 15, 2026. If you want to see Timothée Chalamet's orange "Marty Supreme" suit and other fashion faux pas live, red carpet coverage begins 30 minutes earlier at 6:30 p.m. ET on ABC and Hulu or 4:00 p.m. ET on E! for complete fashionista coverage.

How much does Hulu cost for the Oscars?

Hulu (With Ads) currently costs $11.99/mo., while the No Ads version is $18.99/mo. Both plans include the live Oscars stream. Hulu + Live TV packages are also available starting at $88.99/mo.

Is Conan O'Brien hosting the Oscars again?

Yes, Conan O'Brien is returning for his second year in a row. His 2025 hosting stint was widely considered a return to form for the Academy Awards, leading to his quick rehiring for the 98th ceremony.

The post Hulu Will Stream the 2026 Oscars Live—Will the Feed Drop Before Best Picture Again? appeared first on CableTV.com.

item.title: 6 ‘New on Hulu’ Shows and Movies to Stream in March 2026 (#2 Will Blow Your Mind)
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com Staff
item.description:
From the 98th Oscars livestream to the return of "Mormon Wives" and "Paradise" Season 2, discover the 6 best new shows and movies on Hulu in March 2026

The post 6 ‘New on Hulu’ Shows and Movies to Stream in March 2026 (#2 Will Blow Your Mind) appeared first on CableTV.com.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Randy Harward, our Senior Staff Writer with over 25 years covering entertainment and TV.

March is officially here, and at CableTV.com, we’re keeping a close eye on the Hulu library as it balances gritty prestige dramas with the ultimate comfort viewing. While the Disney+ merger is nearly complete, Hulu is still the reigning champ for next-day air favorites from ABC and FOX. Whether you’re gearing up for the high-stakes glitz of the Academy Awards or looking for some ironic-watch CGI nostalgia, there’s plenty to dig into this month.

6. “Garfield: The Movie” (2004)

Believe it or not, the 2004 live-action/CGI hybrid “Garfield: The Movie” has achieved legendary ironic-watch status in 2026. We’re seeing a massive resurgence of interest in 2000s-era hybrid films on TikTok, and fans are calling this version superior to more recent iterations. The big draw here is Bill Murray’s deadpan delivery, which has become a staple meme for Gen Z. It’s the perfect, low-stress, vibes pick for a lazy Sunday afternoon when you just want to see a fat cat eat lasagna and deal with a dorky Jon Arbuckle.

5. “The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives” (2024–present)

MomTok’s new frontier is finally here with Season 4 dropping all 10 episodes on March 12. This season follows the cast as they navigate their transition into mainstream celebrity life while trying to keep their tight-knit community from unraveling. We’re especially watching Taylor’s journey as she prepares for her transition to “The Bachelorette,” a move Hulu is promoting heavily this month. It’s high-drama, high-stakes, and exactly the kind of reality anchor we expect from Hulu’s spring lineup.

4. “The 98th Oscars” (2026)

Hulu will livestream the 98th Academy Awards ceremony on March 15 at 7:00 p.m. ET. This year, Hulu is even testing a new picture-in-picture feature, so you can watch the red carpet arrivals while simultaneously browsing the “New in March” category for your next bingCe. If you’re a film buff, this is the biggest live event of the season, and having it directly in the Hulu app is a huge plus for cord-cutters who don’t want to miss a single acceptance speech.

3. “The Secret Agent” (2025)

This isn’t the TV series you might be thinking of; this “The Secret Agent” (original title “O Agente Secreto”) is the Oscar-nominated Brazilian masterpiece that’s taking 2026 by storm. Directed by Kleber Mendonça Filho and starring a phenomenal Wagner Moura, it’s a simmering neo-noir political thriller set during the 1977 Brazilian military dictatorship. The film follows a technology researcher on the run in Recife, navigating an environment of surveillance and paranoia during Carnival. It’s landed four Academy Award nominations—including Best Picture and Actor in a Leading Role for Moura—making it essential prestige viewing for March.

2. “Paradise” (2025–present)

Fresh off its late February premiere, Season 2 of “Paradise” is the high-concept thriller you need to be watching this March. Starring Sterling K. Brown, this series has leaned into its “mystery box” roots, introducing new survivors and uncovering the dark secrets of the underground city. It’s the perfect binge for fans of twisty political thrillers who want something a bit more cerebral than your average drama.

1. “Abbott Elementary” (2021–present)

As part of Hulu’s next-day air dominance, new episodes of “Abbott Elementary” continue to be a Wednesday night staple. The mockumentary style remains as fresh as ever as Janine and the crew navigate the hilarious hurdles of the Philadelphia public school system. It’s the ultimate comfort watch that balances sharp social commentary with genuine heart, making it a favorite for educators and comedy fans alike.

See if Hulu is right for you

We’ve been tracking Hulu’s customer satisfaction rating and pricing for years, and while that $18.99 ad-free price point is getting steep, the value of live events like the Oscars makes it a heavy hitter for the spring season. Don’t forget to check out our full Hulu review for the latest 2026 pricing and bundle options before you dive in!

New on Hulu FAQ

How much is Hulu in 2026?

As of March 2026, the Hulu ad-free plan is priced at $18.99 per month. However, many users find better value by opting for the Disney Bundle, which integrates Hulu content directly into the Disney+ app for a more streamlined experience across the Disney and Hulu libraries.

Can I watch the Oscars on Hulu?

Yes! Hulu will be livestreaming the 98th Oscars on March 15, 2026, starting at 7:00 p.m. ET. This year, they are introducing a new "Picture-in-Picture" feature so you can keep an eye on the red carpet while you browse other content or check your Oscar pool results.

What is the stream limit for Hulu?

In early 2026, the two-stream limit remains a standard for Hulu accounts. This has been a point of feedback for many customers who have larger households, though it remains consistent with many other top-tier streaming services looking to manage bandwidth and account sharing.

What to read next

  • Hulu Review: Is It Still Worth It in 2026?
  • What to Watch on Hulu This Week
  • The Best Returning Shows Coming in 2026

The post 6 ‘New on Hulu’ Shows and Movies to Stream in March 2026 (#2 Will Blow Your Mind) appeared first on CableTV.com.
